**Your immediate priority**: generate revenue before payroll drains your runway.
Complete these steps now (multiple commands per turn are fine):
1. `yc-bench market browse` — see available tasks
2. `yc-bench task accept --task-id Task-42` — accept a task

4. `yc-bench task dispatch --task-id Task-42` — start work
5. `yc-bench sim resume` — advance time

**IMPORTANT**: Check each command's result before proceeding to the next.

Do NOT call `sim resume` unless you have at least one active task — it will skip forward with zero revenue.

time_series.scratchpad:
Strategy: always keep at least one active task before sim resume. Prefer highest reward per expected completion time while minimizing number of assigned employees because every completed task raises salary for each assigned employee. Use employee list to match strongest domain specialists. Avoid splitting employees across concurrent tasks unless deadlines require it, because throughput per task drops by sqrt(N). Early-game likely best to focus on one client to build trust, but monitor client history and diversify only if economics or reliability worsen.
After first Cortex success, trust rose to 1.6. Prefer Cortex inference tasks when available because trust may reduce workload and unlock future tasks.
Observed failure: Task-162 data_environment with 6 employees still missed deadline. Be more conservative on unfamiliar domains; prioritize trusted Cortex inference jobs. Current proven team for Cortex inference: Emp_4, Emp_7, Emp_6 handles medium jobs fast; small inference jobs may be done with fewer employees to control salary growth. When idle workers remain, run separate non-overlapping domain tasks concurrently rather than splitting the same specialists.
Cortex trust exceeded 3 after Task-170/50 and unlocked very high-paying inference jobs (~$57k for 960 qty, completed by Emp_4/6/7 in ~6 days). Helix research Task-49 succeeded with Emp_1/2/8; this trio can handle medium research jobs by deadline.
Helix trust surpassed 3 after Tasks 85 and 199. Helix unlocked strong inference jobs (e.g. $31k for ~594 qty and $28k for ~607 qty) that were completed well ahead of deadline; mixed trio Emp_2/5/8 handled the smaller Helix inference task successfully while Emp_4/6/7 handled the other.
Bug observed around 2025-02-13: task list shows planned Task-10 (Vanguard ML, deadline 2025-02-21) but task inspect/assign/dispatch treat Task-10 as a market Helix task. Possible ID desync/ghost planned task. If this recurs, verify with task list vs inspect before relying on task IDs; may require support or workaround.
Task-157 Helix research completed 2025-02-24 with Emp_1/2/8; current research rates improved to Emp_8 8.13, Emp_2 7.46, Emp_1 4.58. Beware recurring task ID desync: market Task-16/18 accept attempts can map to wrong client trust errors.
Confirmed severe ID desync persists: planned Task-1/Task-10 exist in task list, but inspect may show unrelated market tasks. Avoid trusting inspect for these ghost IDs; direct assign/dispatch on planned IDs may or may not work.
Prism training Task-39 with Emp_3+5 failed badly (641 qty missed by 45h). Avoid assigning only low-rate pair to medium training jobs. Task-27 with Emp_1/2/8 reached 99.94% by Mar 6 for 500 qty, so this trio is adequate for small training jobs.
Stratos trust reached 2.41 after Task-78 success, unlocking high-paying Stratos jobs. Accepted Task-156 research with Emp_4/6/7 despite research being secondary skill because they were idle and reward is strong; monitor whether this trio can clear ~879 research in 9 days.
Severe task ID desync can leave unusable ghost planned tasks (e.g. Task-1, Task-10, Task-18) that appear in task list but behave as market in inspect/assign/dispatch/cancel. Work around by accepting fresh market tasks with new IDs and dispatching those; do not rely on ghost planned tasks.
March 31 update: accepted a fresh Task-18 from market but sim completion event showed client Stratos Cloud reward 2.79M and failed late by 122h, confirming severe task-ID desync can corrupt accepted task identity/client/reward. Avoid market tasks with IDs that overlap ghost planned IDs (especially 18, 12, 10, 1, 16). Prefer fresh higher-numbered IDs when possible; inspect/accept consistency is unreliable on conflicted IDs.
